text.title: django-pytest-mocking Decision Tree
text.summary: Per boundary: pick a mocking technique.

decision-tree.root-question: What kind of boundary is this?

branch.conclusion: technique="responses". Use @responses.activate; add expected calls with responses.add(); assert on responses.calls.
technique="unittest-mock-patch". Patch at the CONSUMER's import path (apps.billing.services.stripe), not the definition (stripe).

branch.question: Are you testing that the task RAN, or that it was TRIGGERED?

branch.conclusion: REJECT — never mock the ORM. Use real factories + db fixture.
REJECT — never mock sleep. Patch the rate-limiter or retry decorator directly.

text.example: boundary: apps.billing.services calls stripe.Charge.create q-boundary-kind -> 'sdk-or-python-import' => conclusion r1: patch_target="apps.billing.services.stripe.Charge.create"; assert_called_once_with(amount=..., currency='usd').
